publishedAbstract: In some cases while troubleshooting mds issue in ODF we often need to collect mds debug logs. This article has instructions on how to do so and can be used for troubleshooting mds issues. **NOTE** If it is necessary to perform MDS debugging, please have RH Support confirm that the MDSs are healthy enough for a debug session. For example, in most instances, the MDSs should be in their normal state, which is up:active, and safe for a debug session. However, in some instances, one of the MDSs may be stuck in replay. If the debug session causes the active MDS to failover, this could result in the loss of both MDSs increasing the chances for corruption. **NOTE** Running the below is only for debug purposes. DO NOT keep debug logging enabled for an extended period of time or you run the risk of filling up the filesystem. Furthermore, enabling debug logging will force you to restart the mds service. Please keep this in mind when doing this process.
publishedTitle: How to collect mds debug logs in Openshift Data Foundation
